title: Week in Tech: AI Agents Go Mainstream
summary: AI agents hit enterprise, Apple shakes up the dev ecosystem, and crypto regulation gets real.

stories.title: AI Agents Are Now Enterprise-Ready
stories.take: This is the year agents go from demo to deployment. The tooling finally caught up — expect every SaaS company to ship an agent feature by Q3.

stories.title: Apple Opens Up Sideloading in iOS 20
stories.take: Huge for indie developers. The 30% tax era is ending, slowly. If you're building mobile, this changes your distribution strategy.

stories.title: SEC Proposes Clear Crypto Framework
stories.take: Finally, some regulatory clarity. This is bullish for builders in the space — uncertainty was the real killer, not regulation itself.

stories.title: Vercel Ships Edge-First Database Layer
stories.take: The full-stack JS ecosystem keeps eating. If you're still debating your backend stack, the answer is increasingly 'just use Vercel.'

stories.title: Remote Work Debate Reignites at Big Tech
stories.take: The RTO push is about real estate leases and middle management, not productivity. The data is clear — let builders build from wherever.

content: Welcome to this week's roundup. Five stories that caught my attention, with my unfiltered take on each.
